symptom: drone re-emits search_web with the same query; advisory nudge 'DUPLICATE SEARCH: You already searched for X' fires, but the tool runs again — second API round-trip + identical result content re-dumped into context
root_cause: search_web is in tool_dedup.NO_CACHE_TOOLS (correct — results may be time-sensitive), so the general dedup layer never catches it. The _search_queries set tracked prior queries but only for the advisory nudge, not for dispatch short-circuiting.
fix_approach: purpose-built _search_results_cache dict keyed on query string alone. First call: tool runs, result stored. Second call with same query: short-circuit with '[search_web cached — no round-trip]' prefix + cached content. Only successful results cached (is_error=False).
fix_location: tsunami/agent.py::_step — search_web branch at ~L3010 + post-execution store at ~L3745
principle: v5 — the old nudge asked; the new cache enforces. Time-sensitivity of results is preserved by session-scoped TTL (cache lives for the run, not across runs).

desc: agent.py short-circuits search_web on cached query
cache is populated post-execution on successful runs only
old advisory 'DUPLICATE SEARCH' copy is removed — no add_system_note firing the redundant text
short-circuit emits is_error=False — skip is not a failure
